atisitvā.meaning: having gone too far; having overstepped, gone beyond one’s limits

breakdown: Proceeds, goes on; from √vatt “to turn, proceed”.

ativelaṁ.meaning: too long; beyond the proper time

attabyābādhāyapi.meaning: also to one’s own harm; even to the affliction of oneself
attabyābādhāyapi.gram: masculine noun, dative singular, compound + particle (atta + byābādha + pi)
breakdown: To pain, harm, misery; dative singular of byābādha.

attakilamathānuyogo.meaning: the practice of self-mortification; devotion to self-weariness
attakilamathānuyogo.summary: This compound names one of the two extremes rejected in the Buddha’s first discourse: the pursuit of self-torment.
attakilamathānuyogo.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular, compound (atta + kilamatha + anuyoga)
breakdown: Practice of, devotion to, application (kilamatha + anuyoga coalesces to kilamathānuyoga).

attasammāpaṇidhi.meaning: right aspiration for oneself; setting oneself in the right direction
breakdown: Aspiration, resolve, direction of the mind.

atthabhañjako.meaning: spoiling another’s welfare; breaking the good of others
atthabhañjako.gram: adjective, nominative singular masculine, compound (attha + bhañjaka)

atthaddho.gram: past participle, nominative singular masculine, of na thambhati (na + √thambh)
breakdown: Negative prefix meaning “not” (with sandhi doubling of the following t).
Rigid, obstinate, dull; past participle of thambhati.

atthagacchati.meaning: to disappear; to go out of existence; to set
atthagacchati.gram: verb, present tense, 3rd person singular (atthaṁ + gacchati, “goes home/to setting”)
breakdown: Home, setting (as of the sun); disappearance.

atthakkhāyī.meaning: telling what is beneficial; giving good advice
breakdown: Telling, declaring; from akkhāti “to announce” (attha + akkhāyī coalesces with doubling of the k).

atthakusalena.meaning: by one proficient in the good; skilled in what is advantageous
breakdown: Proficient, skilful; instrumental singular of kusala.

atthakāmā.meaning: wishing welfare; well-wishing, desiring another’s good

atthamaññātuṁ.meaning: to understand the meaning; to grasp the sense
breakdown: To understand; infinitive of ājānāti (the m bridges the vowels in sandhi).

atthangata.meaning: gone home; set (as the sun); disappeared

attharāpesi.meaning: had (it) spread out; caused to be covered
attharāpesi.gram: verb, aorist 3rd person singular, causative of attharati (ā + √thar)

atthasaṁhita.meaning: endowed with benefit; connected with the good
breakdown: Endowed with, connected with; past participle of sandahati.

atthavasaṁ.gram: neuter noun, accusative singular, compound (attha + vasa)
breakdown: Influence, power; in this compound: reason, consequence.

atthaṅgamā.gram: masculine noun (from atthaṁ gacchati “goes to its setting”)

atthi.gram: verb, present tense, 3rd person singular of √as “to be”

atthābhisamayā.meaning: comprehension of the meaning; grasping of what is beneficial
atthābhisamayā.gram: masculine noun, ablative singular, compound (attha + abhisamaya)
breakdown: Comprehension, penetration, realization (attha + abhisamaya coalesces to atthābhisamaya).

atthāya.meaning: for the benefit; for the sake of
atthāya.gram: masculine noun, dative singular of attha

attupanāyiko.meaning: referring to oneself; applied to oneself
attupanāyiko.gram: adjective, nominative singular masculine, compound (atta + upanāyika)
breakdown: Self, oneself (atta + upa- coalesces to attupa-).
Referring to, leading up to; from upa + √nī “to lead”.

attāpi.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular + enclitic particle (attā + api)
breakdown: Self, oneself; nominative singular of atta.

attāpissa.meaning: even of one’s own person; also of his self
breakdown: Of him, of one; genitive singular pronoun.

atītamaddhānaṁ.meaning: in the past; during past time
atītamaddhānaṁ.gram: adverb, accusative singular, compound (atīta + addhāna “stretch of time”)
breakdown: Past, gone by; past participle of accayati (ati + √i).

atītassa.meaning: of the past; of what has gone by
atītassa.gram: past participle of accayati used as noun, genitive singular (ati + √i + ta)
breakdown: Root “to go”; atīta is “gone beyond, past”.

atītassānusocanā.meaning: lamenting over the past; worrying about what has gone
breakdown: Lamenting, grieving over; from anu + √suc (the vowels coalesce to -assānu-).

atītaṁ.gram: neuter noun / past participle of accayati (ati + √i + ta)
breakdown: Root “to go”; the lexicon notes the literal sense “not standing”, what has passed on.
Future, not yet come (atīta + anāgata coalesces to atītānāgata).

avadhi.gram: verb, aorist 3rd person singular of vadhati (√vadh)

avadhī.gram: verb, aorist 3rd person singular of vadhati (√vadh)

avagacchati.meaning: to come to; to approach, visit

breakdown: Prefix: down; here giving the sense of grasping.
Gone; past participle of gacchati; avagata “arrived at, understood”.

avajja.gram: adjective / neuter noun (na + √vajj + ya, “to be avoided”)
breakdown: What should be avoided or blamed; potential participle from √vajj.

avaṅkā akuṭilā ajimhā.meaning: not bent, not crooked, not deceitful; straight and honest

aveccappasādena.meaning: with perfect faith; with unshakable confidence
aveccappasādena.gram: masculine noun, instrumental singular, compound (avecca + pasāda)
breakdown: Fully, perfectly; having understood (gerund of aveti, with sandhi doubling of the following p).
With faith, confidence, serene trust; instrumental singular of pasāda.
Without enmity, without rivals (a- + sapatta “rival, foe”); the m bridges the two words in sandhi.

avibhattā.meaning: not detailed; not divided, not analysed
avibhattā.gram: past participle of na vibhajati (na + vi + √bhaj)
breakdown: Divided, analysed, detailed; past participle of vibhajati.

avicāraṁ.meaning: without sustained application; without moving (of the mind over its object)
breakdown: Sustained application of the mind, examining movement of thought; from vi + √car.

avigatapaccayo.meaning: non-disappearance condition (one of the twenty-four conditions of the Paṭṭhāna)
breakdown: Not disappeared, not gone away (a- + vigata, past participle of vigacchati).

avihiṁsāsaṅkappo.meaning: the thought of non-violence; thought free from cruelty
avihiṁsāsaṅkappo.summary: This compound names one aspect of right thought (sammāsaṅkappa) on the Noble Eightfold Path.
avihiṁsāsaṅkappo.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular, compound (na + vihiṁsā + saṅkappa)
avihiṁsāsaṅkappo.goenka: Avihiṁsāsaṅkappo, the thought of non-violence, is one of the three aspects of right thought (sammāsaṅkappa) on the Noble Eightfold Path, together with the thought of renunciation and the thought free from ill will. When such thoughts are cultivated and repeatedly dwelt upon, the mind becomes predisposed to non-violence; they harm neither oneself nor others, strengthen wisdom and are conducive to nibbāna.
